description: The complete CIP-309 v1 error-code catalogue. Codes are SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE and stable; implementations MUST emit them byte-exact and MUST NOT introduce lowercase synonyms. Part A codes are emitted by the structural validator (a pure function over CBOR bytes — no I/O, no signature verification, no decryption). Part B codes are emitted by the public verifier and recipient verifier (which fetch metadata, verify signatures, resolve URIs, and decrypt). Severities are taken from the frozen severity map: `error` fails the record, `warning` is a non-fatal runtime anomaly, and `info` is a deliberate non-check that does not fail the record. Two codes carry dual severity (`dual_severity: true`): their default reading is `info`, and a strict / merkle-only verifier promotes them to `error`. Additions are additive-only and must ship with conformance vectors.

entries.description: Canonical-CBOR decode failed. A single code covers every decode failure: malformed or truncated bytes, indefinite-length (streaming) encodings, non-canonical (unsorted) map-key ordering, duplicate map keys, non-minimal integers, and invalid UTF-8.
A field is present but has the wrong CBOR type or shape (e.g. a non-map `hashes`, a non-integer Argon2id parameter, or an empty `crit[]`).
A required field is absent (e.g. the record version `v` or another mandatory key).
An unrecognized field appears where the map is closed — an unknown top-level key outside the base set and the extension-key namespaces, or an unknown Argon2id params field.
A field that must equal a fixed literal carries a present-but-wrong value (e.g. the record version `v` set to a value other than the required literal).
The record carries no content commitment: both `items[]` and `merkle[]` are empty or absent. A record MUST carry at least one of them non-empty.
A digest's byte length does not match the length pinned for its algorithm — either a content-hash digest in an item's `hashes` map or a `merkle[]` commitment root.
An item's `hashes` map names a content-hash algorithm that is not in the hash-algorithm registry.
A `merkle[]` commitment names an algorithm that is not in the Merkle-commitment-algorithm registry.

A reconstructed URI is not a valid absolute URI under the profile — it carries a fragment, lacks a scheme, uses a scheme outside {ar://, ipfs://}, or fails the per-scheme shape check (Arweave 43-char base64url txid, or the IPFS CID profile).
A chunk in a chunked byte-string or text array exceeds the maximum per-chunk byte length.

An encryption envelope's `aead` names an unauthenticated cipher — a block-cipher mode with no integrity (CBC, CTR, ECB, CFB, OFB) in any key-size spelling, or a legacy stream/block cipher (RC4, DES, 3DES). Reported instead of the generic UNSUPPORTED_AEAD_ALG so the failure names the integrity hazard precisely.
An encryption envelope's `aead` names a cipher that is not in the content-AEAD registry (and is not in the forbidden unauthenticated-cipher family).
An encryption envelope's `nonce` byte length does not match the length pinned for its `aead` algorithm.
An encryption envelope's `scheme` is not the supported unsigned integer scheme identifier.
An encryption envelope declares `slots` but the array is empty; at least one recipient slot is required.
A recipient slot is not the closed 2-key map the declared KEM requires — it is the wrong type, carries the other KEM's ciphertext field, carries an unexpected key, or is missing a required field.
An encryption envelope's `kem` names a key-encapsulation mechanism that is not in the KEM registry.
An encryption envelope declares `slots` but is missing the `kem` that determines the recipient-slot shape.
A classical-KEM slot's ephemeral public key `epk` has the wrong byte length for its KEM.
A hybrid-KEM slot's KEM ciphertext `kem_ct` does not reassemble to the byte length pinned for its KEM.
A recipient slot's `wrap` is not 48 bytes (32-byte wrapped content-encryption key + 16-byte AEAD tag).
An encryption envelope's `slots_mac` (the MAC binding the recipient-slot set) has the wrong byte length.
An encryption envelope declares `slots` but is missing the required `slots_mac` binding them.
An encryption envelope declares `slots_mac` but carries no `slots` for it to bind.
An encryption envelope combines the recipient-slot key-path (`slots`) with the passphrase key-path (`passphrase`); exactly one key-path is allowed.
An encryption envelope declares neither `slots` nor `passphrase`, leaving no on-chain key-path to recover the content-encryption key.
An item carries an `enc` envelope but its `hashes` map declares no content-hash (sha2-256 / blake2b-256), so the ciphertext is not bound to any plaintext digest.
A passphrase envelope's `alg` names a KDF that is not in the passphrase-KDF registry.
A passphrase envelope's salt is shorter than the minimum salt length.
A passphrase envelope's salt is longer than the maximum salt length.
A passphrase envelope's Argon2id parameters fall below a security floor (memory m < 65536 KiB, time t < 3, or parallelism p < 1).
A passphrase envelope's KDF parameters exceed the upper policy bound (a denial-of-service backstop against unbounded work factors).

A signature entry's COSE_Sign1 (or its inline COSE_Key sidecar) is structurally invalid — fails to decode, carries an attached (non-detached) payload, or its COSE_Key has the wrong key type, curve, or public-key shape.

A signature entry's COSE_Sign1 protected `alg` is not a registered signature algorithm label. Info severity: an unrecognized signature algorithm does not fail the record (signatures are always optional).
A signature entry is not the closed map the schema requires — wrong type, missing required field, or an unexpected key.
A signature entry carries both a 32-byte protected-header `kid` (key-by-reference path) and an inline `cose_key` sidecar (key-by-value path); the two key-resolution paths are mutually exclusive.
A signature entry's inline COSE_Key carries the private scalar `d` (COSE_Key label -4). Publishing private-key material on the permanent ledger is forbidden; this is a producer-side preflight guard.
A `supersedes` transaction reference does not have the required byte length.
The record's `crit[]` names a critical extension that this validator does not implement, so the record cannot be safely processed.
A `crit[]` entry violates the list-shape rules — it names a base key, does not match the extension-key form, is named but absent from the record map, or is duplicated.
No CIP-309 metadata (label 309) was found at the referenced transaction.
The transaction has fewer confirmations than the verifier's threshold. Info severity: the record may still be structurally valid; the depth is reported for the caller to weigh.
A record-level signature failed cryptographic verification against its claimed public key.
A signature's signing key could not be resolved (no inline COSE_Key and no resolvable key-by-reference).
A claimed publishing wallet does not match the address that submitted the transaction.
A URI resolves to a forbidden network target (e.g. a host on the deny-list), blocked to prevent server-side request forgery.
Content fetched from a content-addressed URI does not match the digest the URI commits to.
A URI fetch failed transiently. Warning severity: a non-fatal runtime anomaly (e.g. a gateway error) that does not by itself invalidate the record.
The referenced plaintext content could not be retrieved from any provided URI, so the content hash could not be recomputed.
The sealed-PoE ciphertext could not be retrieved from any provided URI, so decryption could not proceed.
A required external provider (chain explorer or storage gateway) was unavailable for the verification.
Verification could not be completed without trusting a single issuer-controlled service, violating the standalone-verifiability requirement.
The inputs supplied to recipient decryption have the wrong shape (e.g. the wrong slot or envelope structure).
The recipient private key does not unwrap any slot in the envelope; this key is not a recipient of the sealed PoE.
The authenticated encryption-envelope header failed its integrity check (the slot MAC or associated data did not verify).
The content ciphertext failed its AEAD authentication tag check during decryption.
Key derivation from the passphrase (or from the KEM shared secret) failed.
The number of supplied Merkle leaves does not match the leaf count the commitment declares.
The supplied Merkle-leaves payload uses a format the verifier does not support.
The supplied Merkle-leaves payload is structurally malformed.
The Merkle root recomputed from the supplied leaves does not match the committed root.
The Merkle leaves could not be retrieved, so the root could not be recomputed. Warning severity: a non-fatal availability anomaly.
The Merkle leaves were provided only in an informative (non-verifiable) form. Info severity: reported, but not a verification failure.

A Merkle commitment uses an algorithm the verifier does not support. Dual severity: the default reading is info when another commitment in the same record was validated; promoted to error for a merkle-only record (no content claim was validated alongside it).
A check was skipped because its target is out of the verifier's profile. Dual severity: the default reading is info (render mode); a strict end-to-end verifier promotes it to error.
